---
title: "ell_to_dense"
generated: true
description: "Scatter an ELL back to a dense `(m, n_cols)` matrix."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.sparse.ell`](../nitrix-sparse-ell.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:ell_to_dense -->
```python
ell_to_dense(ell: ELL) -> Num[Array, 'm n']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:ell_to_dense -->
Scatter an ELL back to a dense `(m, n_cols)` matrix.

Useful in tests and in fallback paths.  Pad entries (assumed to
carry the algebra's identity) are written via `scatter_add` with
the identity, which is a no-op for `REAL`; callers using
non-additive semirings should not rely on this for round-trip
correctness.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:ell_to_dense -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `ell` | `ELL` | — | — | Sparse matrix in ELL format, with `values` and `indices` of shape `(m, k_max)` and an implicit column count `n_cols`. |

**Returns** `Num` — shape `m` × `n`. Dense matrix of shape `(m, n_cols)` formed by scatter-adding each stored value into its `(row, indices[row, p])` position.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:ell_to_dense -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `m` | rows of a multi-row contrast |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
